dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3140 IS: Flood Protection and Infrastructure Resilience Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Ricketts (for himself and Mrs. Gillibrand) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry

form.official-title: To modify Department of Agriculture programs to improve flood protection and infrastructure resiliency, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Flood Protection and Infrastructure Resilience Act of 2025.
Section 403 of the Agricultural Credit Act of 1978 (16 U.S.C. 2203) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary may undertake measures that increase the level of protection above that which would be necessary to address the immediate impairment of the watershed if the Secretary determines that such restoration is in the best interest of—

paragraph.text: the long-term health of the watershed; and
the protection of the watershed from repetitive impairments.

section.header: Rehabilitation of structural measures near, at, or past their evaluated life expectancy
section.text: Section 14(b) of the Watershed Protection and Flood Prevention Act (16 U.S.C. 1012(b)) is amended by striking paragraph (2) and inserting the following:

subparagraph.text: The amount of Federal funds that may be made available under this subsection to a local organization for construction of a particular rehabilitation project shall be equal to 65 percent of the total rehabilitation costs, but not to exceed 100 percent of actual construction costs incurred in the rehabilitation.

Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), the Secretary may contribute up to 90 percent of the total rehabilitation costs to a local organization for construction of a particular rehabilitation project that serves a limited resource area, as determined by the Secretary, but not to exceed 100 percent of actual construction costs incurred in the rehabilitation.

subparagraph.header: Costs of resource rights and permits
subparagraph.text: The local organization shall be responsible for the costs of water, mineral, and other resource rights and all Federal, State, and local permits.

section.text: Section 1271(b) of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3871(b)) is amended by striking paragraph (2) and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: To address natural resource concerns on eligible land on a regional or watershed scale, including—

subparagraph.text: the conservation, protection, restoration, and sustainable use of soil;
the conservation and protection of water, including sources of drinking water and groundwater;
the prevention and mitigation of the effects of flooding and drought, and the improvement or expansion of flood resiliency; and
the conservation of wildlife, agricultural land, and related natural resources.
